reasoning: The bullish consensus rests entirely on premarket structure (CLEAN_BULL ribbon, +5.56pt overnight gap, VIX falling, XLK +1.07%) that has NOT been tested by real RTH volume. The overnight gap occurred on zero-volume premarket and is historically fragile—gaps set on overnight/premarket liquidity fill 40-60% of the time. More critically, the rally is TECH-ONLY: XLK +1.07% vs XLF +0.04% (flat) and XLE +0.37% (weak). The internals agent flagged this breadth concern but the consensus didn't downweight. When financials don't participate despite a gap-up, rotation risk is acute. PMH 729.39 is only 9 cents above current price; if it fails on first RTH test, mechanical drop to 726.97 (−0.3%) follows. The consensus assumes premarket momentum continues into RTH volume; history shows narrow-breadth overnight gaps collapse in first 30 minutes of real trading. The unanimous bullish call is overconfident on an untested premarket setup.

invalidation_scenarios.scenario: SPY opens within 10 cents of 729.39 and closes below Fast EMA 728.48 within first 5 RTH bars (before 09:45 ET)
invalidation_scenarios.effect: Breaks CLEAN_BULL ribbon on first test, invalidates the core technical thesis. Overnight momentum is revealed as fake; likely triggers gap fill toward 723.74. Ribbon-based confidence collapses.

invalidation_scenarios.scenario: XLF breaks below premarket high by ≥0.5% in first 30 minutes while XLK remains flat or red
invalidation_scenarios.effect: Confirms sector rotation away from risk-on. Tech-only momentum was not sustainable. Breadth divergence like this historically precedes sharp reversal (−0.5 to −1.0% SPY decline). Death knell for the bullish case.

invalidation_scenarios.scenario: VIX spikes above 18.5 within first hour on no major macro catalyst, while price still above 729.00
invalidation_scenarios.effect: Reverses the 'compression bullish' macro narrative. VIX inversion on a rally signals market has repriced to risk-off intraday. Invalidates the VIX-falling confidence pillar. Historical pattern: VIX spike without catalyst = short-term rollover.

weakest_consensus_link: The entire bullish case assumes premarket momentum (CLEAN_BULL ribbon + 5.56pt gap + VIX falling + tech outperformance) will CONTINUE when real RTH volume enters. But premarket structure is untested, overnight gap is low-volume-fragile, and breadth is narrow (tech-only, XLF flat). The moment RTH liquidity tests PMH 729.39, the fragile setup could evaporate into gap fill or rotation.
key_observations: Unanimous 4-of-4 bullish consensus on premarket data alone—no RTH bars tested yet. This is a structural overconfidence risk.
Tech-only breadth (XLK +1.07% vs XLF +0.04%) is the weakest pillar the consensus ignores. Narrow-breadth rallies collapse when financials refuse to participate.

PMH 729.39 is only 9 cents above current price; next support is 2.33 away at 726.97. Asymmetry is asymmetric: quick −0.3% downside, ambiguous upside (round numbers above are psychology, not technical).

Overnight gap +5.56pt occurred on zero-volume premarket. Gaps set in low-volume windows are 2–3x more likely to fill than gaps set on heavy volume. The consensus treats gap as a 'buy signal' when it's structurally fragile.
FOMC is tomorrow (5/7 14:00 ET). Market may be front-running into FOMC, not trading on 5/6 fundamentals. Event risk is higher than macro agent admits.
